scope.notes: T09 implemented the Web create-flow pages against the existing local API client and draft store. Runtime browser screenshots and full product acceptance remain owned by T13-T16.

commands.evidence: PowerShell artifact check parsed T09.json and verified required handoff/log/evidence files exist.

evidence.summary: Verified four create-flow routes no longer use placeholders, required copy and controls exist, API-001..005 client calls are wired, mood guards exist, upload failure can skip, generation needs selected drawPosition, duplicate generation is disabled, and forbidden paywall terms are absent.
Defined UI-001, UI-002, UI-003, UI-004, and UI-010 screenshot target paths for T13/T14/T15 runtime capture.
TypeScript completed with no emit and no errors.
ESLint completed with max-warnings=0 after escaping JSX apostrophes and removing an unused test import.
Route smoke recognizes the new T09 flow components and existing later placeholders.
Existing API client and analytics whitelist unit slice passed after adding click_start_card.

repairRecord.issue: Initial create-flow page test failed because the new test passed a string to assert.match instead of a RegExp.
repairRecord.repair: Wrapped static copy checks in RegExp construction and reran the required page test.

repairRecord.issue: Lint failed on unescaped apostrophes in JSX and one unused test import.
repairRecord.repair: Escaped JSX apostrophes, removed the unused import, normalized visible text in the page test, and reran lint/page/typecheck.

repairRecord.issue: Spec required the home CTA to emit click_start_card but the inherited analytics whitelist lacked that event.
repairRecord.repair: Added click_start_card to the whitelist, emitted it from the home CTA, asserted it in the page test, and reran the analytics unit slice.

rerunResumeState.notes: Use pnpm.cmd on this Windows PowerShell surface. T09 did not start a browser/server or capture real screenshots; runtime click/API transcripts and visual screenshots remain T13-T15 work.

exitCondition: T13 can execute create-stage browser clicks from mood selection through context, upload or skip, draw selection, generation, and navigation to /result/[id].
